narration: Ever wonder why things fall apart? It’s called entropy.
visual_description: Hook visual: Ever wonder why things fall apart? It’s called entropy.
ai_image_prompt: Hook visual: Ever wonder why things fall apart? It’s called entropy.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Hook visual: Ever wonder why things fall apart? It’s called entropy.

narration: In physics, entropy measures disorder. The universe naturally favors chaos over neatness.
visual_description: Context visual: In physics, entropy measures disorder. The universe naturally favors chaos over neatness.
ai_image_prompt: Context visual: In physics, entropy measures disorder. The universe naturally favors chaos over neatness.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Context visual: In physics, entropy measures disorder. The universe naturally favors chaos over neatness.

narration: Imagine a tidy room. Over time, it becomes a chaotic mess. That’s entropy in action.
visual_description: Escalation visual: Imagine a tidy room. Over time, it becomes a chaotic mess. That’s entropy in action.
ai_image_prompt: Escalation visual: Imagine a tidy room. Over time, it becomes a chaotic mess. That’s entropy in action.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Escalation visual: Imagine a tidy room. Over time, it becomes a chaotic mess. That’s entropy in action.

narration: The second law of thermodynamics states that total entropy can never decrease. It’s a one-way street.
visual_description: Evidence visual: The second law of thermodynamics states that total entropy can never decrease. It’s a one-way street.
ai_image_prompt: Evidence visual: The second law of thermodynamics states that total entropy can never decrease. It’s a one-way street.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Evidence visual: The second law of thermodynamics states that total entropy can never decrease. It’s a one-way street.

narration: This principle isn’t just theory. It drives engines, powers reactions, and defines the flow of time itself.
visual_description: Payoff visual: This principle isn’t just theory. It drives engines, powers reactions, and defines the flow of time itself.
